Province: * The province/territory where the hospital is located.

† CTAS levels I–III (discharged) cases include patients with a triage level of resuscitation, emergent or urgent and a visit disposition of discharged home; they exclude patients who were transferred to another facility and those who died.

‡ CTAS levels IV–V (discharged) cases include patients with a triage level of less urgent or non-urgent and a visit disposition of discharged home; they exclude patients who were transferred to another facility and those who died.
§ Admitted cases include patients who were admitted to the reporting facility and do not include patients who were transferred to another facility.
** Information presented here includes only facilities that submitted data to NACRS in 2024–2025 at the time of data extract. Results comparing jurisdictions should be interpreted with caution.
Records with invalid length of stay and/or unknown triage level are included in the total number of ED visits.
In 2024–2025, NACRS included all EDs in Quebec, Ontario, Alberta and the Yukon, 2 in P.E.I., 8 in Nova Scotia, 16 in Manitoba, 61 in Saskatchewan and 30 in B.C.
National Ambulatory Care Reporting System, 2024–2025, Canadian Institute for Health Information.
